outlook_paragraph: CRGY shows moderate drilling activity with 11 active sites at decision date, 4.0 above the recent average, indicating a pickup in operational momentum heading into year-end 2022. The relatively low segment fraction of 20% implies only partial exposure to the observed Permian activity, tempering the revenue upside despite positive drilling signals. With WTI averaging near $67.50 and realized prices at an 87% discount factor, the deterministic revenue forecast settles at approximately $596.2M for the quarter. Newly active pads suggest some expansion, though activity remains concentrated in a limited subset of sites.
